warfarin.details: Hold 5 days pre-op. Check INR day before surgery (target <1.5). Bridge with LMWH if high thrombotic risk (mechanical valve, recent DVT/PE <3mo, AFib with prior stroke).
warfarin.resume: Resume evening of POD 0-1 if adequate hemostasis

apixaban.details: Hold 48 hours pre-op (72 hours if CrCl <25 mL/min or high-bleed-risk procedure). No bridging required.
apixaban.resume: Resume 48-72 hours post-op when hemostasis is confirmed

rivaroxaban.details: Hold 48 hours pre-op (72 hours if CrCl <30 mL/min or high-bleed-risk procedure). No bridging required.
rivaroxaban.resume: Resume 48-72 hours post-op when hemostasis is confirmed

dabigatran.details: Hold 48 hours pre-op if CrCl >50 mL/min; hold 4-5 days if CrCl <50 mL/min. Reversal agent idarucizumab (Praxbind) available for emergencies. No bridging required.
dabigatran.resume: Resume 48-72 hours post-op when hemostasis is confirmed

enoxaparin.details: Hold therapeutic dose 24 hours pre-op; hold prophylactic dose 12 hours pre-op. Check anti-Xa level if renal impairment.
enoxaparin.resume: Resume prophylactic dose 12-24 hours post-op; therapeutic dose 48-72 hours post-op if hemostasis confirmed

aspirin.details: Continue for patients with cardiac stents or high cardiovascular risk. For primary prevention or non-cardiac indications, discuss with surgeon; may hold 7 days pre-op if bleeding risk outweighs cardiac benefit.
aspirin.resume: Continue perioperatively for cardiac patients; restart POD 0-1 if held

clopidogrel.details: Hold 5 days pre-op for elective surgery. Do NOT discontinue within 6 months of bare-metal stent or 12 months of drug-eluting stent placement without cardiology consultation.
clopidogrel.resume: Resume POD 1 with loading dose if appropriate, per cardiology guidance

prasugrel.details: Hold 7 days pre-op for elective surgery. Do NOT discontinue within 12 months of drug-eluting stent placement without cardiology consultation.
prasugrel.resume: Resume POD 1 with loading dose if appropriate, per cardiology guidance

ticagrelor.details: Hold 5 days pre-op for elective surgery. Do NOT discontinue within 12 months of drug-eluting stent placement without cardiology consultation.

metformin.details: Hold morning of surgery. Risk of lactic acidosis with contrast dye or renal hypoperfusion. Hold 48 hours if IV contrast planned.
metformin.resume: Resume when tolerating oral intake and renal function is stable post-op

insulin_glargine.details: Reduce dose by 20-25% the evening before or morning of surgery. Do NOT hold completely to avoid DKA in Type 1 diabetics. Monitor blood glucose every 1-2 hours perioperatively.
insulin_glargine.resume: Resume home dose when eating normally; use sliding scale insulin until then

insulin_lispro.details: Hold on morning of surgery (mealtime insulin not needed while NPO). Cover with sliding scale as needed perioperatively.
insulin_lispro.resume: Resume when patient is eating regular meals post-op

glipizide.details: Hold morning of surgery. Sulfonylureas cause hypoglycemia during fasting. Monitor blood glucose perioperatively.
glipizide.resume: Resume when tolerating oral intake post-op

glyburide.details: Hold morning of surgery. Longest-acting sulfonylurea with highest hypoglycemia risk. Consider holding evening before surgery. Monitor blood glucose.
glyburide.resume: Resume when tolerating oral intake post-op

empagliflozin.details: Hold 3-4 days pre-op. SGLT2 inhibitors carry risk of euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) perioperatively. Check ketones if glucose >250 or patient symptomatic.
empagliflozin.resume: Resume when eating normally and clinically stable, typically POD 2-3

dapagliflozin.details: Hold 3-4 days pre-op. SGLT2 inhibitors carry risk of euglycemic DKA perioperatively. Check ketones if glucose >250 or patient symptomatic.
dapagliflozin.resume: Resume when eating normally and clinically stable, typically POD 2-3

semaglutide.details: Hold 7 days (weekly injection) pre-op per ASA 2023 guidance. GLP-1 agonists delay gastric emptying, increasing aspiration risk under anesthesia. Consider gastric ultrasound if not held.
semaglutide.resume: Resume at next scheduled weekly dose when tolerating oral intake

liraglutide.details: Hold day of surgery. Daily GLP-1 agonist that delays gastric emptying, increasing aspiration risk. Consider gastric ultrasound if not held appropriately.
liraglutide.resume: Resume when tolerating oral intake post-op

lisinopril.details: Hold morning of surgery. ACE inhibitors may cause refractory hypotension under anesthesia. Continue if primary indication is heart failure with reduced EF.
lisinopril.resume: Resume POD 1-2 when hemodynamically stable and euvolemic

enalapril.details: Hold morning of surgery. ACE inhibitors may cause refractory hypotension under anesthesia. Continue if primary indication is heart failure with reduced EF.
enalapril.resume: Resume POD 1-2 when hemodynamically stable and euvolemic

losartan.details: Hold morning of surgery. ARBs may cause refractory hypotension under anesthesia. Continue if primary indication is heart failure with reduced EF.
losartan.resume: Resume POD 1-2 when hemodynamically stable and euvolemic

valsartan.details: Hold morning of surgery. ARBs may cause refractory hypotension under anesthesia. Continue if primary indication is heart failure with reduced EF.
valsartan.resume: Resume POD 1-2 when hemodynamically stable and euvolemic

metoprolol.details: Continue perioperatively. Abrupt withdrawal risks rebound tachycardia, hypertension, and myocardial ischemia. Cardioprotective in perioperative setting.
metoprolol.resume: Continue without interruption; give with sip of water morning of surgery

atenolol.details: Continue perioperatively. Abrupt withdrawal risks rebound tachycardia and myocardial ischemia. Administer morning of surgery with sip of water.
atenolol.resume: Continue without interruption; convert to IV if unable to take PO

carvedilol.details: Continue perioperatively. Abrupt withdrawal risks rebound tachycardia and worsening heart failure. Administer morning of surgery with sip of water.
carvedilol.resume: Continue without interruption; use alternative IV beta-blocker if unable to take PO

atorvastatin.details: Continue perioperatively. Statins have pleiotropic anti-inflammatory and plaque-stabilizing effects that are cardioprotective in the surgical setting.

rosuvastatin.details: Continue perioperatively. Statins have pleiotropic anti-inflammatory and plaque-stabilizing effects that are cardioprotective in the surgical setting.

simvastatin.details: Continue perioperatively. Statins have pleiotropic anti-inflammatory and plaque-stabilizing effects that are cardioprotective in the surgical setting.

st_johns_wort.details: Stop 5 days pre-op. Induces CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, reducing efficacy of many anesthetic and perioperative drugs (midazolam, fentanyl, cyclosporine, warfarin).
st_johns_wort.resume: May resume post-op when no longer taking interacting medications

ginkgo_biloba.details: Stop 7 days pre-op. Inhibits platelet-activating factor (PAF) and may potentiate bleeding, especially when combined with anticoagulants or antiplatelets.
ginkgo_biloba.resume: May resume when surgical wound is healing and hemostasis is confirmed

garlic_supplement.details: Stop 7 days pre-op. Inhibits platelet aggregation and may increase bleeding risk. Also potentiates effects of anticoagulants and antihypertensives.
garlic_supplement.resume: May resume when surgical wound is healing and hemostasis is confirmed

ginger.details: Stop 7 days pre-op. May inhibit thromboxane synthetase and platelet aggregation, increasing bleeding risk perioperatively.
ginger.resume: May resume when surgical wound is healing

fish_oil.details: Stop 7 days pre-op. High-dose omega-3 fatty acids may impair platelet aggregation and prolong bleeding time, though recent evidence suggests modest risk.
fish_oil.resume: May resume when surgical wound is healing

ephedra.details: Stop at least 7 days pre-op. Sympathomimetic effects cause tachycardia, hypertension, and arrhythmias. Risk of hemodynamic instability and myocardial ischemia under anesthesia.
ephedra.resume: Strongly discourage resumption; discuss risks with patient

kava.details: Stop 7 days pre-op. Potentiates GABAergic sedation and may prolong effects of anesthetics. Associated with hepatotoxicity.
kava.resume: May resume post-op; counsel patient on hepatotoxicity risk

valerian.details: Stop 7 days pre-op. GABA-ergic mechanism may potentiate sedative effects of anesthetics. Abrupt withdrawal after chronic use may cause benzodiazepine-like withdrawal.
valerian.resume: Taper gradually if chronic use; may resume post-op if no interactions with current medications

furosemide.details: Hold morning of surgery to avoid intraoperative hypovolemia and electrolyte imbalances. Check potassium and magnesium pre-op. Continue if needed for active heart failure management.
furosemide.resume: Resume POD 0-1 when volume status and electrolytes are assessed

albuterol.details: Continue perioperatively. Bring inhaler to hospital. Administer pre-op nebulizer treatment for patients with reactive airway disease or active wheezing.
albuterol.resume: Continue without interruption; ensure available in PACU

levothyroxine.details: Continue perioperatively. Long half-life (7 days) means missing one dose has minimal clinical impact, but routine continuation is preferred. Take morning of surgery with sip of water.

prednisone.details: Continue perioperatively. Do NOT abruptly discontinue due to adrenal suppression risk. Patients on >5 mg/day for >3 weeks may need stress-dose steroids (hydrocortisone 50-100 mg IV) for major surgery.
prednisone.resume: Continue home dose; taper stress-dose steroids over 1-3 days post-op if given
